methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $182M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Fuse Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Standard Nuclear, a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Kurt Terrani, operates within a sector comprising 3 public peers (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private competitors including Fuse Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power. The company has raised $182M to date.

A peer-median valuation approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient clustering within narrow comparables—only one same-stage and one adjacent-stage nuclear peer exist with sufficient data, falling short of the three-datapoint minimum required for reliable median triangulation. Accordingly, stage-progression methodology was applied as the primary valuation anchor. Using total capital raised ($182M) as a value proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $364M.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $309M to $419M, centered on $364M. This median reflects the growth profile consistent with Series A deployment of significant capital in frontier nuclear technology. Key uncertainties include (1) the absence of sufficient narrowly comparable private financings to validate peer-derived multiples, (2) the wide dispersion of development stages and technical approaches across the nuclear energy competitor set, and (3) limited public market liquidity for sector benchmarking. The range should be treated as preliminary pending deeper discounted cash flow modeling or closing of additional narrow peer transactions.
